commentaries.author: Ignatius of Antioch
commentaries.content: Salute thou also Mary my daughter, distinguished both for gravity and erudition, as also "the Church which is in her house."

commentaries.author: John Chrysostom
commentaries.content: Greet the brethren who are in Laodicea, and Nymphas, and the church which is in his house. See how he cements, and knits them together with one another, not by salutation only, but also by interchanging his Epistles. Then again he pays a compliment by addressing him individually. And this he does not without a reason, but in order to lead the others also to emulate his zeal. For it is not a small thing not to be numbered with the rest. Mark further how he shows the man to be great, seeing his house was a church.
